Title: Microalgal glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase role in galactolipids and high-value storage lipid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: acylates glycerol-3-phosphate at the sn-1 position using acyl-CoA to form lysophosphatidic acid in the eukaryotic Kennedy pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: endoplasmic reticulum-associated eukaryotic Kennedy pathway supplying precursors for galactolipid and TAG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for phosphatidic acid, DAG, galactolipids, TAG, and OPO assembly
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed under nitrogen-replete and nitrogen-depleted conditions; experimentally increased in OE and reduced in KD lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased galactolipids (especially MGDG), TAG, biomass, and OPO content/yield under nitrogen deficiency; knockdown reduced galactolipids under nitrogen depletion and impaired growth.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GPAT family acyltransferase; ER-associated membrane acyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous yeast expression and enzyme assay; regiospecificity assay; subcellular fractionation/immunoblotting; overexpression; knockdown; RT-qPCR; Western blot; lipidomics by UPLC-MS/MS; TAG GC-MS profiling; deuterated PA feeding
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for eukaryotic Kennedy pathway contribution to galactolipid, TAG, and OPO biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrGPATer encodes an ER/lipid droplet-associated sn-1 glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase that prefers acyl-CoA donors, especially 16:0-CoA. 2. Overexpression increased MGDG and DGDG accumulation under nitrogen depletion, while knockdown decreased galactolipids, demonstrating involvement in galactolipid biosynthesis. 3. Overexpression enhanced TAG accumulation, biomass, and OPO content/yield, supporting its utility for functional lipid engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising metabolic engineering target to enhance TAG and OPO production in microalgae; could be stacked with other ER glycerolipid enzymes such as CrLPAAT2 and synthetic OPO modules to improve structured lipid manufacture.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown impaired growth and photosynthetic performance under nitrogen depletion; overexpression showed no obvious reported growth penalty and increased biomass.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast thylakoid membranes for MGDG/DGDG; lipid droplets for TAG/OPO
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: galactolipids are major thylakoid membrane lipids; TAG is storage lipid; OPO is a high-value human milk fat substitute for infant formula
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study supports that ER-derived eukaryotic Kennedy pathway intermediates contribute to chloroplast galactolipid synthesis in C. reinhardtii; potential sterol acyltransferase-like activity remains unresolved.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: presumed acylation of glycerol-3-phosphate in the prokaryotic/chloroplast Kennedy pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies chloroplast precursor pool for glycerolipid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: tested under nitrogen-replete and nitrogen-depleted conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown did not significantly alter MGDG or DGDG contents; expression increased in CrGPATer KD lines, suggesting compensatory involvement in TAG homeostasis rather than primary galactolipid control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockdown; RT-qPCR; galactolipid profiling by UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CrGPATcp is the chloroplast/prokaryotic pathway GPAT isoenzyme. 2. Its transcript increased in CrGPATer KD lines, consistent with compensation between eukaryotic and prokaryotic pathways. 3. CrGPATcp knockdown did not significantly change MGDG or DGDG contents, indicating it is not the main determinant of galactolipid accumulation in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority target for galactolipid engineering than CrGPATer; may participate in compensatory TAG homeostasis under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast thylakoid membranes for galactolipids; lipid droplets for TAG
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: galactolipids support thylakoid membrane structure; TAG stores carbon/energy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a directly tested isoenzyme comparator in Results; despite pathway relevance, its knockdown had little effect on galactolipid levels under the conditions used.
